newsSince: No material company-specific news since June 9. The Apple American Manufacturing Program inclusion and Face ID design win (GlobalFoundries Malta NY) were announced March 26, 2026; FY26/Q4 results came May 6 and a Stifel Buy June 4 - all pre-June 9 and already reflected. The -4.2% move since June 9 is flow.

sources.label: Apple newsroom - American Manufacturing Program partners (Mar 26)
CRUS Apple manufacturing role / Face ID - Yahoo Finance

verdicts.rankRationale: On the AI-node bottleneck lens CRUS remains peripheral: no HBM/advanced-packaging exposure and the Apple Face ID/manufacturing news (Mar 26) doesn't address data-center AI nodes and is pre-June 9 anyway. No new evidence since June 9; removal case from prior verdict stands. Hold near the bottom.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Apple Face ID IC ramp (2028-29) - but not AI-node relevant
verdicts.primaryRisk: ~90% Apple concentration; no direct AI-node bottleneck role
verdicts.bull: Apple Face ID design win and US-manufacturing partner status deepen the AAPL relationship with multi-year ASP upside.
verdicts.neutral: Real franchise but consumer-audio/mixed-signal, not an AI compute bottleneck; story unchanged since June 9.
verdicts.bear: Zero direct HBM/packaging/CPO relevance plus extreme Apple concentration; belongs off an AI-node alpha list.

verdicts.rankRationale: Through the co-packaged-optics lens CRUS has essentially no data-center optical content; the Apple/Face ID news is consumer mixed-signal and pre-June 9. No new CPO-relevant evidence since June 9, so the prior remove case is unchanged. Hold.
verdicts.bull: Best-in-class mixed-signal IP could touch future optical-adjacent SerDes, but speculative.
verdicts.neutral: Quality analog name, but no co-packaged-optics exposure to justify CPO-list inclusion.
verdicts.bear: Low data-center optical exposure plus Apple concentration; cleaner CPO bottleneck names dominate.
